title: Critical CPU Utilization Spike on Application Cluster
symptoms: CPU utilization at 98% across all search service ECS tasks
Search API response times exceeding 12 seconds (normal: 150ms)
Thread dumps showing regex engine consuming all CPU in tight loops
Specific search query patterns triggering catastrophic regex backtracking
ECS task health checks failing due to response time degradation
root_cause: Catastrophic regex backtracking in search query parser caused by nested quantifiers in a recently deployed regular expression pattern
resolution: Rolled back the search service to the previous version without the problematic regex, deployed a fix with an optimized non-backtracking regex pattern, and added regex execution timeout
preventive_actions: Add regex complexity analysis to code review checklist
Implement regex execution timeout of 100ms in search parser
Add CPU utilization alerting at 80% with automatic scaling
Include regex performance testing in search service CI pipeline
Deploy regex static analysis tool to prevent catastrophic patterns

timeline.event: CPU utilization spikes to 98% on search service tasks
Nina Patel begins investigation, notes recent deployment
Thread dumps reveal regex engine consuming all CPU
Problematic regex pattern identified in new query parser
Service rolled back to previous version
CPU utilization drops to normal, search recovering
All search metrics normalized, incident resolved

embedding_summary: CPU spike to 98% on search service ECS tasks caused by catastrophic regex backtracking in recently deployed query parser. Search response times degraded from 150ms to 12 seconds for 30 minutes affecting 9,000 EU users. Resolved by rolling back deployment and deploying optimized regex pattern.
